title: String error: no known conversion from 'VlWide<5>' to 'initializer_list<char>' for 1st argument
body:
Hello,

I wrote a basic SystemVerilog class to make my prints prettier and well formatted. Until now I always used it with Icarus but now I'm using more and more Verilator, I try to use it with it too. However, I experience an error I don't understand:


```
...
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/usr/include/c++/v1/string:822:5: note: candidate constructor not viable: no known conversion from 'VlWide<5>' to 'const std::string &' for 1st argument
    basic_string(const basic_string& __str);
    ^
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/usr/include/c++/v1/string:827:5: note: candidate constructor not viable: no known conversion from 'VlWide<5>' to 'std::string &&' for 1st argument
    basic_string(basic_string&& __str)
    ^
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/usr/include/c++/v1/string:840:5: note: candidate constructor template not viable: no known conversion from 'VlWide<5>' to 'const char *' for 1st argument
    basic_string(const _CharT* __s) : __r_(__default_init_tag(), __default_init_tag()) {
    ^
...

```

I'm using Verilator 4.226 on macOS Monterey 12.4. You can reproduce this issue to have more messages by cloning 'develop-verilator' branch and go to example folder and execute `./runme.sh --simulator verilator`. The repository is here: 

[SVLogger](https://github.com/dpretet/svlogger/tree/develop-verilator)

The code consist of a simple class in this [file](https://github.com/dpretet/svlogger/blob/develop-verilator/svlogger.sv)

Could you help me please to understand the problem and if I made a mistake somehow with Verilator.

Damien
